name: Vía Verde de Poblete — round trip
name_original: Vía Verde de Poblete — ida y vuelta
region_intro: Jogging around Campo De Calatrava offers a distinctive experience within a unique volcanic landscape in Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. The region features over 300 individual volcanic structures, including maars and cinder cones, providing varied terrain from sedimentary plains to moderately high ranges. Joggers can explore routes near volcanic lakes and wetlands, such as the Caracuel Lagoon and Laguna del Prado, often framed by Mediterranean vegetation like holm oaks and junipers. The area also integrates historical sites, allowing for a blend of physical activity and cultural immersion. Best jogging routes around Campo De Calatrava The most popular jogging route is Vía Verde de Poblete — round trip, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 29 minutes to complete, offering an easy path along a greenway. Another top favourite among local runners is Camino Manchego de Santiago and La Atalaya Park Loop, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.8 km) path that combines historical routes with parkland scenery. Local runners also love the Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through a scenic lagoon area, often completed in about 28 minutes. Jogging around Campo De Calatrava is defined by its volcanic landscapes, tranquil wetlands, and Mediterranean mountains, offering a mix of challenging and more gentle paths. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to moderate trails with varied elevation. The routes in Campo De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1,400 runners have used komoot to explore Campo De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Campo de Calatrava?
A: Komoot offers over 160 running routes in Campo de Calatrava, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore the region's unique volcanic landscapes.

Q: What makes jogging in Campo de Calatrava unique?
A: Jogging in Campo de Calatrava offers a distinctive experience due to its unique volcanic origins. The region features over 300 individual volcanic structures, including maars (water-filled craters) and cinder cones. You can run through varied terrain, from sedimentary plains to moderately high ranges, often alongside volcanic lakes and wetlands like the Caracuel Lagoon and Laguna del Prado, framed by Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Campo de Calatrava?
A: The running routes in Campo de Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse volcanic landscapes, tranquil wetlands, and the blend of challenging and gentle paths. Many appreciate the opportunity to combine physical activity with historical immersion.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Campo de Calatrava?
A: Yes, there are several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210330588/via-verde-de-poblete-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Poblete — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) trail along a greenway, ideal for a gentle run. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211472556/laguna-del-prado-circular-by-pozuelo-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, a scenic 2.5-mile (4.0 km) path.

Q: Can I find challenging long-distance running trails in the region?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Campo de Calatrava offers several longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar of La Hoya del Mortero – Desire Line... loop from Ciudad Real central&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.7-mile (15.7 km) route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210119907/los-castillos-del-convento-de-calatrava-la-nueva-y-de-salvatierra-circular-por-la-sierra-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Castillos del Convento de Calatrava la Nueva y de Salvatierra — circular por la Sierra de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) trail with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural attractions along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region beautifully integrates historical and natural sites. You can run past landmarks linked to the Order of Calatrava, such as the Calatrava la Nueva fortress, or explore the Alarcos Archaeological Park. Natural attractions include numerous volcanic lakes and wetlands like the Caracuel Lagoon Nature Reserve and the Peñarroya Volcano and Lagoon Natural Monument. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209250698/camino-manchego-de-santiago-and-la-atalaya-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Manchego de Santiago and La Atalaya Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, combine historical paths with parkland scenery.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints in Campo de Calatrava?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic viewpoints, especially those traversing the moderately high ranges and volcanic structures. The region's diverse relief, including Mediterranean mountains, provides elevated spots for panoramic views of the unique volcanic landscape. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5298526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – La Atalaya MTB Trails loop from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt; offers great vistas.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Campo de Calatrava?
A: Yes, many running routes in Campo de Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211472556/laguna-del-prado-circular-by-pozuelo-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210109854/natural-path-of-the-guadiana-through-the-el-vicario-reservoir-circular-through-peralvillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural path of the Guadiana through the El Vicario Reservoir — circular through Peralvillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Campo de Calatrava?
A: While specific rules can vary by protected area or private land, generally, many natural trails in Campo de Calatrava are dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry waste bags to maintain the natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Campo de Calatrava?
A: The best time for running in Campo de Calatrava is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be quite hot, making early mornings or late evenings preferable, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there parking facilities available near the running trails?
A: Yes, many running trails in Campo de Calatrava have parking facilities nearby, especially those starting from towns or popular natural areas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking availability at the trailhead.

Q: Are there running routes accessible by public transport in Campo de Calatrava?
A: Some running routes in Campo de Calatrava, particularly those closer to larger towns like Ciudad Real, may be accessible via public transport. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblete Greenway – Maar of La Hoya del Mortero loop from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt; start near urban centers, making them potentially reachable by local bus services.

Q: Are the running trails in Campo de Calatrava family-friendly?
A: Yes, Campo de Calatrava offers several family-friendly running routes, especially the easier, flatter paths like greenways or trails around lagoons. These routes provide a safe and enjoyable environment for families to run together, often with beautiful natural surroundings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210330588/via-verde-de-poblete-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Poblete — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example of a family-friendly option.
tips: A path that patiently takes us to the ruins of the Castillo de la Ciruela, through cultivated fields and beautiful landscapes. Good area for walking or cycling.
The Vía Verde was carried out by an agreement with RENFE, using the traces of the old train tracks linking Ciudad Real with Poblete. They are a little less than 6 km.
